content:
🚀 modly | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Generating 3D models from images using local AI processing.
✨ Key Features: • AI-powered image-to-3D mesh generation • Runs entirely on local GPU • Open-source desktop application • Supports external AI model extensions • Available for Windows and Linux
📖 Summary: Modly is an open-source desktop application that leverages local AI models running on your GPU to generate 3D models from 2D images. It supports an extension system for integrating various AI models and is available for Windows and Linux, with macOS support planned.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 plain-app | Kotlin
🎯 Primary Use Case: Securely managing and accessing an Android phone's data and features from a web browser on the same local network, without relying on cloud services.
✨ Key Features: • Web-based phone management (no desktop app needed) • 100% local and end-to-end encrypted communication • File browsing, upload, download, rename, delete • Media viewing, streaming, and management
📖 Summary: PlainApp is an open-source Android application that provides a secure, self-hosted web interface for managing your phone from any device on the same network. It offers a comprehensive suite of tools for file management, media browsing, communication, and even screen mirroring, all without relying on cloud services.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 unifi-docker | Shell
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide an easy-to-deploy and manage Unifi Network Controller instance using Docker, abstracting away underlying system dependencies and complexities.
✨ Key Features: • Dockerized Unifi Controller • Simplified installation and upgrades • Version and dependency management • Cross-platform compatibility (Ubuntu, Debian, macOS, Windows, Raspberry Pi) • Persistent data storage outside the container
📖 Summary: This repository provides a Dockerized version of Ubiquiti's Unifi Controller, simplifying installation, management, and upgrades. It bundles the controller, Java, and OS dependencies into a single container, ensuring a consistent environment across various operating systems and hardware.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 TaxHacker | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Automating the extraction and organization of financial data from receipts and invoices using AI for accounting and tax purposes, with a focus on self-hosting and data privacy.
✨ Key Features: • AI-powered data extraction from receipts and invoices (OCR) • Automatic categorization of transactions
📖 Summary: TaxHacker is a self-hosted AI-powered accounting application designed to automate expense and income tracking for freelancers and small businesses. It leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) to extract data from receipts and invoices, supports multi-currency conversions, and allows for custom data extraction through user-defined prompts.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 TradingAgents | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To develop and deploy autonomous AI agents for automated financial trading and market analysis.
✨ Key Features: • Multi-agent LLM financial trading framework • Support for various LLM providers (OpenAI, Gemini, Claude, Grok, etc.) • Structured output agents for research, trading, and portfolio management • Backtesting capabilities with date fidelity • Persistent decision logging and LangGraph checkpoint resume
📖 Summary: TradingAgents is a Python framework designed for multi-agent financial trading using Large Language Models (LLMs). It enables the creation of autonomous trading agents that can research markets, make trading decisions, and manage portfolios.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
